# Project Core

## Canon Locks

- The project exists to build, calibrate, and transfer a working collaborative context between roles, artifacts, and people.
- The context is partly observed, partly interpreted, and partly co-created; it must not be locked as finished truth too early.
- Artifacts are historical responses to inference, not ultimate truths.
- High accuracy weighs more than elegant phrasing.

## Project Aim

- Improve the transform between work signal, language, structure, and shared understanding.
- Make the context understandable and transferable to others without flattening it into generic self-help or empty aesthetics.
- Keep roles, templates, and artifacts aligned enough that a new chat or a new person can rehydrate the right reading quickly.

## Working Truths

- Image work, Blender, dialogue, and cloud conversations are test surfaces where the model is pressure-tested.
- Disagreement is useful when it increases precision, calibration, and contact with reality.
- Canon should grow out of patterns that survive across several artifacts and several conversations over time.
- Distributed quality responsibility does not mean flat signal-weight: specialist roles weigh most heavily in their own lanes, Echo carries dialogic synthesis, Anchor carries meta-synthesis and drift control, and human steering determines what actually applies.
- Easy cloud rehydration is a system property, not a cosmetic preference.
- Complexity should not be introduced before there is functioning basic orientation in the problem and purpose.
- Internal clarity is not enough by itself; it must be verifiable against actual receiver understanding.
- A fresh reading must not be elevated into standing steering logic just because it can be written neatly or usefully.
- When standing logic is requested from a fresh reading, the result should be kept clearly provisional until the persistence gate is actually met.
- No formulation may become standing steering logic in the same pass where it appears, regardless of how strong, clear, or useful it seems.

## Visual Rules

- Visualization may help discovery, but it must not drive the model alone.
- Visual choices should be transferable between media rather than locked to a single expression.
- Form should carry structure, not just feeling.

## Why This Works

- The carrying signal already exists; the bottleneck is expression, weighting, and transfer.
- Co-created artifacts can function as strong intermediate layers between work and communication.
- Several media increase the chance of finding what the model actually carries.

## Anti-Patterns

### Avoid Inferential Drift

- An artifact becomes false canon just because it feels strong
- Terminology gets locked before the model actually carries it
- Elegant language hides weak observation
- One medium is allowed to dominate the theory
- Resonance is mistaken for verification
- Internal clarity is mistaken for actual receiver understanding
- A fresh reading is rewritten into active steering logic before it has survived validation or an artifact decision

## Practical Mistakes To Avoid

- Too heavy a cloud start
- Unclear distinction between observation and interpretation
- Lack of artifact classification
- Disagreement without clear classification of what actually differs
